extraction.one_line_summary: GIRMAY Biniam won stage 2 sprint after taking bonus seconds in the tiso kilometer intermediate sprint, beating DÉMARE and VAN AERT
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL contested intermediate sprint bonuses despite being sprinter-unsuited, revealing early GC time-gap anxiety on flat stages.
Jumbo-Visma controlled final classified climb despite no sprint leader, showing team depth allocation on transition stages.
VAN AERT launched sprint at 310m, unusually early for his profile, suggesting reduced explosive power or tactical error.

rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: first win since February crash during classics, demonstrated full form recovery before Tour debut
rider_signals.SCHMID Mauro: neutral: took intermediate sprint bonus seconds in tiso kilometer, showing opportunistic positioning on home roads
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: contested tiso kilometer bonus seconds despite non-sprinter profile, signaling GC micro-management approach
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: neutral: retained leader's jersey without defending, suggesting team saving energy before mountain stages
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: fifth place after returning from altitude training, better-than-expected sprint legs on final Tour de Suisse
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: third place after launching at 310m suggests diminished finishing speed compared to peak form
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: neutral: second place confirms consistent positioning but inability to beat GIRMAY at full power
